subsection.name: &sect-num;.1 History
subsection.p: BreakTest started as an independent fork of Apache JMeter 5.6.3. The goal was to keep the familiar JMX test-plan model while modernizing the runtime, user interface, HTTP stack, scheduling model, diagnostics, and script-repair workflow for current performance-testing teams.

BreakTest is a derivative work of Apache JMeter and retains the required copyright, license, notice, package, and compatibility references. BreakTest is independent from The Apache Software Foundation and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by The Apache Software Foundation.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.2 The Future
subsection.p: BreakTest development is focused on making local script creation, debugging, and validation faster while keeping a clear path to scaled execution and deeper analysis through BreakTest Enterprise.

ul.li: Continue improving HTTP/2 and modern HTTP behavior.
Reduce memory and CPU overhead in large tests and large JMX files.
Improve open and closed workload modeling, pacing, pause/resume, and schedule visualization.
Expand visual diagnostics for recorded-vs-replayed traffic, correlation, assertions, and source navigation.
Make AI-assisted script repair more reliable and easier to review from the GUI.
Keep compatibility with existing JMeter JMX files where practical, while documenting intentional differences clearly.

subsection.note: You can help by opening issues and pull requests in the BreakTest GitHub repository. See Building and Contributing to BreakTest.
